_readme: Manually-added walks with no external source — admin-created via the WalksAdminPanel '+ New walk' button. Entry shape matches data/rambler-walks.json so the build + API pipeline treats them uniformly. Slug convention: `manual-{id}` where {id} is the walk's own 4-char id.

walks.ratingExplanation: Perhaps an idiosyncratic love of mine, as this isn't a popular hiking spot, but I find it exceptionally charming. This route takes you through what little remains of the once-thriving Roman city of Calleva Atrebatum, including the original walls, and a large amphitheatre where gladiatorial combats entertained up to 7,000 people at a time
walks.name: Mortimer to Bramley via Roman Silchester

walks.ratingExplanation: Spoiled by a long trek along a busy road near Audley End
walks.name: Newport to Audley End via Saffron Walden

walks.miscellany: It's too apparent that you haven't left London
walks.name: Coulsdon to Redhill via the Chaldon Way

walks.miscellany: No lunch stops on route. Plenty in Crowborough itself

walks.terrain: verdant coastal cliffs, rolling hills, suburban streets
walks.miscellany: Begins, necessarily, with a lengthy suburban segment, but then becomes very beautiful and surprisingly quiet. Particularly lovely on the walk along the cliffs east of Folkestone, where there are also a number of lunch spot choices. The narrow overgrown paths are best. But note that there is a LOT of steep ascents and descents on this walk.

sights.name: Battle Abbey
sights.description: the site of the Battle of Hastings, upon which William the Conqeuror built his mighty Battle Abbey in papally-imposed contrition for the blood he had shed

walks.terrain: ancient trees, rolling chalk downlands, lush meadows
walks.miscellany: Main paths can be very busy on weekends so take the minor paths to avoid the crowds
walks.ratingExplanation: The Ashridge Estate is very beautiful if you appreciate woodland, and covers more then enough ground to cover a day of hiking in itself. There are paths everywhere and you can wander freely

It's an easy walk from the station to the beautiful, vast (and free) grounds of the finest example of English baroque architecture. For a somewhat extortionate fee you can also visit the house, but you'd probably want a whole day for that

sights.name: Standen House
sights.description: late 19th Century; large and beautiful; filled with Arts and Crafts designs.

walks.name: Amberley to Arundel along the Arun

walks.terrain: immense white chalk cliffs, sea, marshes, meadows, beachfront
walks.miscellany: Best on sunny high summer days when the chalk is gleaming. Seven Sisters extremely busy on Summer weekends/bank holidays
walks.ratingExplanation: Spectacular white cliffs in a well-protected national landscape

walks.privateNote: took a while to get out of the suburbs of Tunbridge, but it was worth

walks.name: Aspley Guise Circular via the Woburn Estate

walks.miscellany: Penultimate section of the Pilgrim's Way to Canterbury
Relatively busy area. Plenty of lunch venues in Goring
walks.privateNote: plenty of places to eat in Goring

walks.terrain: escarpments, three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys

walks.suffix: via the Ox & Obelisk Ponds of Windsor Great Park
walks.name: Windsor Circular via Windsor Great Park

walks.privateNote: Found a nice place to walk

walks.ratingExplanation: Mostly just a succession of unremarkable fields
walks.name: Amersham Circular via the Misbourne Valley

walks.miscellany: Much of the walk is through St Albans, but it's a beautiful city so this is no bad thing

walks.privateNote: We made it better by extending the the walk to Harlow. but still merely 'okay'
walks.name: Bishop’s Stortford to Sawbridgworth

walks.ratingExplanation: Pleasant but nothing spectacular except for Cambridge's architecture

walks.miscellany: Have lunch a café or pub next to the river. Return from Stonehenge to Salisbury by bus (very frequent & reliable but [check times](https://www.connectingwiltshire.co.uk))
Can be EXTREME mud in Lymington Reeds Beds Nature Reserve

walks.name: Potters Bar Circular via Gobions Wood
walks.ratingExplanation: Almost worthy because of the great and tranquil beauty of Gobions Wood, and it being only 20m from Kings Cross on a quick train. Unfortunately the surrounding fields and busy commuter roads that make up the bulk of the area don’t have enough going for them to put this on the recommendation list
